item.title: Microsoft Purges StegoAd Edge Extensions as libssh2 CVE-2026-55200 PoC Emerges
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft Removes 119 Edge Extensions That Hid Malware in Images and Fonts — Must Read

Microsoft removed 119 malicious Edge extensions, with up to 2.6 million installs, that hid payloads in images and fonts to evade detection. The StegoAd campaign used the extensions for ad fraud, credential and 2FA theft, cookie exfiltration, and remote code execution, so affected users should remove them and change sensitive passwords.

  • Microsoft removed 119 malicious extensions from the Edge Add-ons store and suspended more than 90 developer accounts tied to the operation.
  • Microsoft calls the campaign StegoAd and says the extensions hid payloads inside ordinary image and font files while posing as tools such as ad blockers, VPNs, translators, and video downloaders.
  • The 119 extensions had an install base of up to 2.6 million users, but Microsoft says this is a ceiling rather than a confirmed victim count.
  • Microsoft found that retrieved payloads supported ad fraud, remote JavaScript execution, Google credential and second-factor code theft, WordPress admin login harvesting, and bulk cookie exfiltration.
  • Microsoft advises users to compare their installed Edge add-ons against the published extension ID list and to change passwords and review sign-in activity if anything matches or was removed automatically.

Public PoC Released for Critical libssh2 CVE-2026-55200 Client-Side SSH Flaw — Should Read

A public PoC has been released for CVE-2026-55200, a critical libssh2 client-side flaw that could let a malicious SSH server corrupt memory and potentially execute code on connecting clients. Organizations should urgently identify bundled or static libssh2 copies and apply builds containing the upstream patch until an official fixed release is available.

  • A public proof-of-concept has been released for CVE-2026-55200, a critical libssh2 flaw that can let a malicious or compromised SSH server trigger memory corruption on a connecting client with possible code execution.
  • The flaw affects every libssh2 release up to and including 1.11.1 and has a CVSS 4.0 score of 9.2.
  • The bug is in ssh2_transport_read() in transport.c, where an attacker-controlled packet_length field was not checked against an upper bound before 32-bit size calculations could wrap around.
  • There is no fixed libssh2 release yet, but the upstream patch in commit 97acf3d rejects packet_length values above LIBSSH2_PACKET_MAXPAYLOAD, and downstream projects are backporting it.
  • The article recommends inventorying all software that links libssh2, including static or bundled copies, applying a patched build, and restricting outbound SSH connections to trusted servers until patched.

item.title: KDDI Email System Breach Exposes Up to 14.2 Million ISP Logins
Gmail Legal-Notice Phishing Uses short.gy to Hide Credential-Harvest Domain
KDDI Email Breach Exposes 14.2 Million Logins Amid Phishing Campaign Using Gmail Shortlinks for Credential Harvesting
Russian SMS Phishing Targets Messaging Accounts; Clean GitHub Repos Abuse Claude Code
OpenAI GPT-5.6 Sol Preview and Healthcare Invoice Phishing via SaaS Redirects
Russian SMS Credential Theft Campaign, AI Agent Malware via Clean Repo, GPT-5.6 Sol Rollout, and Healthcare Phishing Chain
